summary: Excellent shotgun audience tape of the May 2 1981 Spectrum show, highlighted by a scorching Scarlet > Fire and soaring Bird Song.
review: The recording captures the May‑81 Philadelphia Spectrum concert with crystal‑clear shotgun‑mic detail, only a brief early‑Bird‑Song level bump. Musically the band is on fire – Jerry’s noodling shines, the second set blazes through Scarlet > Fire and nonstop Bird Song, while a few vocal slips and a sloppy Terrapin keep it from perfection.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance praised across most reviews and a consistently lauded shotgun audience recording, with only minor vocal or song‑specific criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Shotgun‑mic (Nak 300) capture delivers clear dynamics and vivid soundstage; early Bird Song levels were a bit saturated but overall crisp with good mix; occasional slight speed variance noted on Scarlet > Fire.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with Sugaree and Jack Straw, building into a fiery second half highlighted by Scarlet > Fire and nonstop energy; vocal slips later in the night temper an otherwise high‑octane performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for spirited noodling and colorful jams, though one reviewer notes a forgotten lyric line during Bird Song.
